title: CodeGenerationSkill
description: Generates production-ready Python, Apex, transformation rules, and migration scripts (v2.0.0)

task_description.description: Plain-language description of the code to generate. Include expected behaviour, error handling requirements, and edge cases.

target_module.description: Dotted Python module path (required when output_type=python). E.g. migration.adapters.salesforce.bulk_poller

apex_class_name.description: Apex class name (required when output_type=apex). Must be PascalCase.

context_files.description: Paths to existing source files the generator should use as context (interfaces, models, error classes, etc.)

entity_names.description: Entity or object names relevant to the generated code (e.g. Account, Contact)

generated_code.description: Complete, syntactically valid source code ready to be persisted

file_path.description: Recommended file path relative to the repository root

imports.description: Ordered list of import statements required by generated Python code. Empty for other languages.

test_suggestions.description: Human-readable test case suggestions to achieve adequate coverage

guardrail_violations.description: List of guardrail violations found. Non-empty means generation FAILED and generated_code must not be used.

title: CodeGenerationSkill Envelope

guardrails.name: no_exec_eval
guardrails.description: Generated code must not use exec(), eval(), compile(), or equivalent dynamic execution

guardrails.name: no_shell_injection
guardrails.description: Generated code must not call subprocess with shell=True or construct shell commands from untrusted input

guardrails.name: no_hardcoded_secrets
guardrails.description: Generated code must not contain API keys, passwords, tokens, or credential literals

guardrails.name: type_hints_required
guardrails.description: All public Python functions must have PEP 484 type annotations

guardrails.name: authorised_paths_only
guardrails.description: File writes must be scoped to /var/data/migration/ or /tmp/migration-work/

guardrails.name: apex_bulkification
guardrails.description: Apex code must operate on collections (List, Set, Map), not individual records in loops

guardrails.name: soql_injection_safe
guardrails.description: Apex SOQL must use bind variables, not string concatenation

input.task_description: Implement a retry-aware Salesforce Bulk API v2 job poller that polls every 30 seconds, raises SalesforceJobFailedError on terminal failure, and returns a BulkJobResult dataclass on success.

output.generated_code:
"""Salesforce Bulk API v2 job poller."""

import asyncio
from dataclasses import dataclass

from migration.adapters.salesforce.errors import SalesforceJobFailedError
from migration.adapters.salesforce.models import BulkJobResult


async def poll_bulk_job(
    job_id: str,
    client: object,
    poll_interval_seconds: int = 30,
    max_wait_seconds: int = 3600,
) -> BulkJobResult:
    """Poll a Salesforce Bulk API v2 job until terminal state."""
    elapsed = 0
    while elapsed < max_wait_seconds:
        status = await client.get_job_status(job_id)
        state = status.get("state")
        if state == "JobComplete":
            return BulkJobResult(
                job_id=job_id,
                records_processed=status.get("numberRecordsProcessed", 0),
            )
        if state in ("Failed", "Aborted"):
            raise SalesforceJobFailedError(f"Bulk job {job_id} entered state {state!r}")
        await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval_seconds)
        elapsed += poll_interval_seconds
    raise TimeoutError(f"Bulk job {job_id} did not complete within {max_wait_seconds}s")
test_suggestions: Test that poll_bulk_job returns BulkJobResult when SF API reports JobComplete.
Test that SalesforceJobFailedError is raised when SF API reports Failed or Aborted.
Test that poll_bulk_job retries until max_wait_seconds before raising TimeoutError.
Test behaviour when SF API returns HTTP 429 (rate-limited) mid-poll.
